content.problemStatement:
Write a program that uses a for-each loop to print the square of each element in list of NNN space separated integers, but skips elements greater than 101010.

Check the sample input and output below for further clarity.

**Note**: Output the square of each element on a new line.
constraints: Each integer fits in a 32‑bit signed range
content.inputFormat: A single line containing space‑separated integers. Each integer is treated as an independent test case.
content.outputFormat: For every integer whose value is less than or equal to 10, output its square on its own line, preserving the original order. No output is produced for integers greater than 10.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The numbers are processed in order. 2, 10 and 6 are ≤10, so their squares 4, 100 and 36 are printed. 12 and 15 are skipped.
5, 7, 10 and 0 satisfy the condition and their squares are output. 11 is ignored.
All numbers are greater than 10, therefore nothing is printed.

solution.approach: Read all integers from standard input one by one. For each integer x, check if x ≤ 10. If the condition holds, compute x·x and print the result on a new line. The algorithm uses a single pass over the input, so it runs in linear time with constant extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) where N is the number of integers in the input.
